testCases.title: Anonymous user cannot publish
testCases.actual: status=401, body={"error":"Missing or invalid Authorization header"}

testCases.title: Deploy dialog opens on clicking Deploy button
testCases.command: Click Deploy button in editor toolbar
testCases.expected: Modal opens with Live/Test environment selector and Deploy button
testCases.actual: Deploy Project modal appeared with Live (Production deploy) and Test (Preview deploy) options, Deploy to Live button visible

testCases.title: Deploy to Live triggers publish flow
testCases.command: Click Deploy to Live in Deploy Project modal
testCases.expected: Server creates deployment, SSE stream connects, progress shown
testCases.actual: API POST /projects/{id}/publish returned 404 Not Found. UI showed misleading ENOENT filesystem error: 'Failed to deploy to /var/lib/doable-sites/dev-vite-updated-zpnwx/live: ENOENT: no such file or directory, mkdir /var/lib/doable-sites'

testCases.title: Deploy to Test (preview) triggers build flow
testCases.command: Select Test environment, Click Deploy to Test
testCases.expected: Build progress shown with steps: Preparing files, Building project, Deploying to preview
testCases.actual: Build progress dialog appeared correctly (Preparing files green, Building project in progress, Deploying to preview pending), then failed with same ENOENT error: /var/lib/doable-sites directory missing on server

testCases.title: Published project has subdomain and published_url fields
testCases.actual: status=published but subdomain=null, published_url=null in API response

testCases.title: Subdomain prefix pattern dev-<slug>.doable.me
testCases.command: Observe generated subdomain from deploy error message
testCases.actual: Generated subdomain was dev-vite-updated-zpnwx (from error message) which follows dev-<slug> pattern correctly. PASS for naming convention.
testCases.notes: Naming convention correct but deploy itself fails

testCases.title: Environment selector in Deploy dialog works
testCases.command: Click Test option in Deploy Project modal
testCases.expected: Test selected, button changes to Deploy to Test
testCases.actual: Test selected (blue highlight), button correctly changed to Deploy to Test, Live deselected

testCases.title: Published project badge shown in dashboard
testCases.command: Navigate to dashboard, observe project cards
testCases.expected: Published projects show Published badge with live URL chip
testCases.actual: Smoke Vite Updated shows Published badge. However no live URL chip visible. Dashboard shows only 1 of 5 projects on Recently Viewed tab.

testCases.title: Monthly credits display shows correct value
testCases.expected: Monthly credits shows used / total (positive numbers)

testCases.title: Login rate limit includes Retry-After header
testCases.actual: 429 returned but no Retry-After header exposed in CORS headers (code sets it server-side but not accessible cross-origin). Rate limit window is 15 min with max 10 attempts.

testCases.title: API health endpoint accessible
testCases.actual: Initially 200 healthy (database up, latencyMs=1). Later went 502 Bad Gateway (API server crashed/restarted during test session)

testCases.title: /dashboard URL renders dashboard
testCases.actual: Intermittently redirects to /billing or /usage instead of /dashboard

testCases.title: Project preview renders in editor
testCases.actual: Preview pane renders correctly showing Doable branded app (Doable - Dream it. Build it. / From zero to wow.)

testCases.title: Runtime metrics endpoint accessible
testCases.actual: status=200 polling every ~5 seconds. Active dev servers = 0 at health check.

testCases.title: Non-owner cannot publish (no auth)
testCases.actual: status=401 Missing or invalid Authorization header

criticalInfrastructureIssues: POST /projects/{id}/publish returns 404 - route not registered on dev API server OR /var/lib/doable-sites directory missing causing deploy to fail server-side
API server went 502 Bad Gateway during test session at ~17:58 UTC
/var/lib/doable-sites directory does not exist on dev server - all deploys fail
